# QPB v1.5.3 — REQUIREMENTS (curated bootstrap)

*Generated by `bin/skill_derivation/curate_requirements.py` from `quality/phase3/pass_c_formal.jsonl`'s 1007 disposition=accepted REQs. The curation algorithm (per Phase 5 DQ-5-7): group by (source_document, section_idx); Jaccard-dedup at 0.6 threshold within each partition; cap at K REQs per partition with K-iteration to land in [80, 110]. See `bin/skill_derivation/curate_requirements.py` for the full algorithm.*

## Coverage by section

| REQ-PHASE3-127 | Check Dependency Manifest to Determine Available Libraries | Check the project's dependency manifest (`requirements.txt`, `build.sbt`, `package.json`, `pom.xml`/`build.gradle`, `go.mod`, `Cargo.toml`) to see what's actually available | 2 |

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-338 | Spec Requirements Tests Cite the Spec Requirement | Each test's documentation cites the spec requirement it verifies. | 2 |

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-678 | Quality suite copies the fixture pattern used by existing tests | Whatever pattern the existing tests use, copy it. | 2 |

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-706 | Fitness-to-Purpose Scenarios section must contain 5–10 scenarios | 5–10 scenarios | 2 |

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-771 | Boundary test body must mutate fixture to trigger defensive code path | Mutate fixture to trigger the defensive code path — present in all six language templates as a required test-body step | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §63 — Structure: Three Test Groups

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-912 | Organize Tests Into Three Logical Groups | Organize tests into three logical groups using whatever structure the test framework provides — classes (Python/Java), describe blocks (TypeScript/Jest), traits (Scala), or subtests (Go) | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §64 — Test Count Heuristic

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-920 | Medium-sized project test suite falls in 35–50 range | For a medium-sized project (5–15 source files), this typically yields 35–50 functional tests. Significantly fewer suggests missed requirements or shallow exploration. | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §65 — Import Pattern: Match the Existing Tests

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-922 | Read Existing Test Files Before Writing Any Tests | Before writing any test code, read 2–3 existing test files and identify how they import project modules. | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §66 — Create Test Setup BEFORE Writing Tests

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-942 | Inline test data must use temp directory support and literal data structures | Create test data directly in each test function using the framework's temporary directory support and literal data structures. | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §67 — No Placeholder Tests

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-949 | Mark Untestable Defensive Patterns with a Comment Instead of a Placeholder | If you genuinely cannot write a meaningful test for a defensive pattern (e.g., it requires a running server or external service), note it as untestable in a comment rather than writing a placeholder. | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §68 — Read Before You Write: The Function Call Map

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-954 | Tests must not use guessed function parameters | The agent explores the architecture, understands conceptually what a function does, then writes a test call with guessed parameters. The test fails because the real function takes `(config, items_d... | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §69 — Writing Spec-Derived Tests

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-960 | Test Must Include Traceability Annotation Citing Spec Section | Each test should include a traceability annotation (via docstring, display name, or comment) citing the spec section it verifies, e.g., `[Req: formal — Design Doc §N] X should produce Y` | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §70 — What Makes a Good Functional Test

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-965 | Tests must target the behavioral layer the requirement describes, not an underlying mechanism | Test the behavior you care about. If the requirement is 'invalid data doesn't produce wrong output,' test the pipeline output — don't just test that the schema validator rejects the input. | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §71 — Cross-Variant Testing Strategy

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-967 | Use parametrization to run assertions across all variants | Use your framework's parametrization mechanism (e.g., @pytest.mark.parametrize, @ParameterizedTest, test.each, table-driven tests, iterating over cases) to run the same assertion logic across all v... | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §72 — Anti-Patterns to Avoid

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-977 | Avoid Catching Exceptions Instead of Checking Output | Testing that code crashes in a specific way isn't testing that it handles input correctly. Test the output. | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §73 — Testing at the Right Layer

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-978 | Tests must verify spec-defined output, not implementation mechanism | Test the spec, not the implementation. The spec says 'invalid data should not appear in output' — not 'validation layer should reject it.' | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §74 — Fitness-to-Purpose Scenario Tests

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-981 | Fitness-to-purpose tests include traceability annotation citing the scenario | Each test should include a traceability annotation citing the scenario, e.g., `[Req: formal — QUALITY.md Scenario 1]`. | 2 |

### references/functional_tests.md §75 — Boundary and Negative Tests

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-984 | Each Boundary Test Includes a Traceability Annotation Citing the Defensive Pattern | Each test should include a traceability annotation citing the defensive pattern, e.g., `[Req: inferred — from function_name() guard] guards against X`. | 2 |

|---|---|---|---:|

### references/iteration.md §76 — Iteration cycle

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-994 | Each iteration strategy targets a distinct bug class | gap finds bugs in uncovered subsystems; unfiltered finds bugs that structure suppresses; parity finds inconsistencies between parallel implementations; adversarial recovers Type II errors from prev... | 2 |

### references/iteration.md §77 — Shared rules for all strategies

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-1009 | Demoted candidates must record all required fields | For each demoted candidate, record: DC-NNN: [short title], Source, Dismissal reason, Code location, Re-promotion criteria, Status: DEMOTED \| RE-PROMOTED [iteration] \| FALSE POSITIVE [iteration] | 2 |

### references/iteration.md §78 — Meta-strategy: `all` — run every strategy in sequence

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-1033 | Single agent session cannot run multiple strategies | A single agent session cannot run multiple strategies (context budget), so `all` is implemented by the orchestrator agent or benchmark runner as a loop of iteration calls | 2 |

### references/iteration.md §79 — Strategy: `gap` (default) — find what the previous run missed

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-1050 | Gap strategy targeted deep-read limited to 2-3 sections | For only the 2–3 thinnest or most gap-rich sections, read the full section content from the previous EXPLORATION.md | 2 |

### references/iteration.md §80 — Strategy: `unfiltered` — pure domain-driven exploration without structural constraints

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-1067 | Unfiltered step 3 answers four domain-knowledge questions | Complete these questions using the code you just explored AND your domain knowledge: API surface inconsistencies, ad-hoc string parsing, domain-expert inputs, silently wrong metadata or configurati... | 2 |

### references/iteration.md §81 — Strategy: `parity` — cross-path comparison and diffing

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-1077 | Perform resource lifecycle parity check for each parallel group | Resource lifecycle parity: Compare what setup/init does with a resource vs. what teardown/reset/cleanup does with the same resource. Every resource acquired in setup must be released in teardown — ... | 2 |

### references/iteration.md §82 — Strategy: `adversarial` — challenge the previous run's conclusions

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-1109 | Define thin-evidence SATISFIED verdicts as single-line, no trace, or grouped | For each requirement the code review marked SATISFIED with thin evidence (single-line citation, no code-path trace, or grouped with 3+ other requirements under one citation): Re-verify the requirem... | 2 |

|---|---|---|---:|

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|

|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-1245 | RIGHT Boundary Tests Assert That Rest of Processing Still Works | expect(output).toContain(expectedType) / assert expected_type in output / assertTrue(output.contains(expectedType)) — 'Rest still works' assertion present in RIGHT test pattern | 2 |

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |

### references/schema_mapping.md §114 — Rules for Choosing Mutation Values

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-1267 | Object/struct field mutations use language-idiomatic zero or empty values | Objects/structs: Python `{}`, Java `new Obj()` with missing fields, Scala `copy()` with `None`, TypeScript `{}`, Go zero-value struct, Rust `Default::default()` or builder with missing fields | 2 |

### references/schema_mapping.md §115 — When to Skip This Step

| REQ ID | Title | Acceptance criteria | Tier |
|---|---|---|---:|
| REQ-PHASE3-1269 | Skip Schema Mapping When No Validation Layer Exists | If the project has no schema validation layer (data flows directly into processing without type checking), you can skip the mapping and use any mutation values. | 2 |
